report.summary: 185.220.101.45 is a confirmed Tor exit node actively used as botnet C2 infrastructure. All five applicable threat intelligence sources independently flag this IP: AbuseIPDB returns a perfect 100/100 score from 94 reports, ThreatFox attributes it to Mirai and Hajime botnets with 100% confidence, and GreyNoise classifies it as an active malicious mass-scanner. Any inbound or outbound connections to this IP in your environment should be treated as a confirmed incident until proven otherwise.
key_findings: AbuseIPDB: 100/100 abuse score from 94 reports across 31 distinct reporters — highest possible confidence rating
ThreatFox: two confirmed C2 entries on ports 9001 and 9030, attributed to Mirai and Hajime botnets at 100% confidence
GreyNoise: classified malicious mass-scanner (noise=true, classification=malicious) — actively scanning the internet
Shodan: ports 9001/9030 confirm active Tor OR/Directory listener; nginx on 80/443 provides web cover
52 OTX pulses from independent researchers corroborate Mirai, Hajime, and Mozi malware family associations

mitre_techniques.justification: Tor exit node used to anonymize C2 communications between infected bots and operator
nginx on 80/443 used to blend malicious traffic with legitimate web traffic
IP is confirmed Mirai botnet C2 relay as documented in ThreatFox and OTX

recommended_actions: Block 185.220.101.45 at perimeter firewall immediately — no legitimate business communication requires a Tor exit node
Search SIEM for all outbound connections to 185.220.101.45 over the last 90 days, focusing on ports 9001 and 9030
If connections found: isolate the source endpoint and initiate incident response — potential Mirai/Hajime infection
Search EDR telemetry for Mirai/Hajime IoCs on any host that connected to this IP
Add to IDS/IPS watchlist and configure alerting for the full /24 subnet 185.220.101.0/24
